If you make some Double Aluminium Plates, you can make a new 96,000L single block tank.
Monsters spawning in or near your base can be annoying. Especially if you happen to get infernal creepers. Luckily, there's a good way to prevent them from spawning at all.

Tired of searching all over the map for that Bamboo Forest or Tropical Rainforest? Desperate for clay dust and need to find a Canyon or Mesa biome? Nature's compass will lead you to the biome you're looking for, if it's within its range.

The compass menu has the following functionality:
* When Sorted By: Name, you can filter the list by name or its parts. The search is case-insensitive.
* You can search by keys and their combinations. For instance, name: bamboo, climate: warm, bh: -1.8, tag: forest.
All available keys are:
name,
climate [hellish, hot, warm, normal, cold, icy],
baseheight | bh (float, can be negative),
heightvariation | hv (float),
rainfall | rain (float),
temperature | temp (float),
humidity [damp, normal, arid],
tags.

Some things to keep in mind:
* Biomes from other dimensions show up on the list, don't bother searching for those unless you're in that dimension.
* In general, the full list has biomes that are not generated by Realistic World Generator, like Sunflower Plains.
* Radius 10000 means it couldn't find the biome. In this case after the initial search, its menu will show only the biomes in the 10k radius. Use Shift+RMB to see the whole list again.
* You can try again at a different location - the range is 10k from your current position.
* Artificially created biomes count, so remove them if you want to find a natural one.
* There's lots of different 'versions' of biomes under the same category, so if you want a tropical one for tropical bees, there are actually a bunch of places you can get it.
* For those who don't mind the freeze when searching, the radius can be changed in a config. The HUD position can also be changed in the config.
After you build a cutting machine you can make some diamond plates for your new 64,000L single block tank.

Firestone ore is used to make some BC machines like the filler, and some other stuff. Unfortunately, it's in the nether...at the bottom of the lava ocean!

Here are some tips for finding it:

-Firestone only spawns in deep lava, at least 6 deep. It will only replace netherrack, so a fjord filled with soulsand won't have any. Can you think of ways to check the lava depth, and what's down there?
-Alternatively, if you keep your head at just the correct height above the lava and at the correct angle, you'll be able to see underneath it - surely there must be a hovering jetpack?
-...or just look for the biome when your cursor is over the JM map. Hell is the default one that has netherrack.

Firestone can be mined with the GT miners, if you place it within horizontal range. Firestone ore and products will cause random fires to start nearby, so get it into a backpack quickly!

A feat of human engineering at your disposal!

This machine allows you to filter 9 items, and will only output set batches. This allows you to automate processes such as electrolyzing that require different amounts of inputs that may clog the system otherwise.

[note]This will be particularly useful in MV once you unlock more electrolysis recipes![/note]

By adding Hydrogen to Sulfuric Light Fuel in a Chemical Reactor you can produce Light Fuel with the very high burn value of 305,000 EU per cell. It is also the main component of Diesel, an excellent fuel that becomes available in MV

The remaining Hydrogen Sulfide can be used to produce Sulfuric Acid for, well, tons of stuff. You can never have enough of it! You can also crack Light Fuel in a Chemical Reactor and then distill it to get Ethylene, for Polyethylene. But Refinery Gas is better for that.

Not really required, but it is a lot faster than the steam one and it can be automated. If you feel like ore washing is too slow, use this hammer.

[note]You can also consider using the steam multiblock instead if you want.[/note]

Nodes can take many upgrades. For now, try building a World Interaction Upgrade. With it, you can set up simple autocrafting.

Put a WIU in a transfer node attached to a crafting table. On the other side of the table, the 3x3 area is now treated as a crafting grid. Place items in inventories on the other side and if they make a valid recipe, the WIU will craft it. To speed it up, you will need to wait until HV for stainless steel.

Also, if you place a node on a block of cobble that has lava and water next to it, and a WIU in it, it will produce cobble for you automatically. Use upgrades to get more at a time and faster.

[warn]The WIU can NOT be used for infinite water in GTNH.[/warn]

Why waste a full ingot to make a rod by using a tool which loses durability and takes even MORE ingots? With this little device, all your rod-needs shall be fulfilled (for now at least, the MV Extruder is the next step for rods).

Oilsand is another way to get heavy oil. Maybe you got lucky and found some in a mountainous biome. With your centrifuge you're able to get heavy oil and sand out of the ore. The veins can be found between Y 50-80.

Sadly, you can't use heavy oil until MV, so just stick them in a barrel or something until then.

[note]Until MV it can only be used for lubricant and burning in a semifluid generator.[/note]

Sometimes you don't want a 3x3 area chunkloaded, but a long straight line instead. This can be handy for rail lines or power lines going across long distances. An anchor sentinel can perform this function. Right-click on a chunk loader with a crowbar, and it will activate. Go in any straight line from the chunkloader and place the anchor and hit it with the crowbar. Those chunks, up to 25x1, will now remain loaded.

This will draw an amount of chunkloader time relative to the 9 chunk area normally loaded. If for some reason you wanted only a 1 chunk area loaded, this will extend the time. Stock up on those pearls or server coins!

If you want to save the environment you can autoclave 16 plantballs to get raw bio fiber with the following liquids:

Biomass 33% chance
Methanol 50% chance
Fuel 90% chance
Nitro-diesel 100% chance

§rHowever, making carbon plates this way will require an electric blast furnace.

Probably the most awesome way to transport just about anything in multiple different ways. Get some conduits of each type. Higher tiers of each (except item) exist.

[note]There's also ME conduits for when you can do that.[/note]

Glycerol is a byproduct of making bio diesel, but it's really useful too. It's used to make epoxid in EV, and for making dynamite, I guess. Both TNT and iTNT are better though.

[note]PS: You don't actually need dynamite, and epoxid can be made another way, if you don't want to make bio diesel forever/at all.[/note]
The lowest tier of renewable, burnable fluid. Generates 60 RF/t, but can be automated for "green" infinite power.

The most efficient way to process Ruby Ore. Better than electrolysis.

[note]Check out NEI for similar processes based on Sapphire and Green Sapphire.[/note]

Some materials like stainless steel require 480 EU/t. Your LV hatches are not good enough. Craft a minimum of two MV hatches to upgrade your electric blast furnace to HV.

[note]BTW, if a Multiblock's recipe is 1EU over 1A for that tier, you must use a second energy hatch for it to use 2A or more.[/note]
MV Energy hatches need ultra low power ICs. Go and craft some.

EV circuits need an MV Circuit Assembler, so make one.

[note]Shortly after this, all circuit recipes will need a cleanroom, so get started on it![/note]

Of course... zombies love that... disgusting liquid (nutrient distillation). While using a small amount per hit, he will attack everything that is within his range with a weapon you give him. That also includes players, so be careful. Rumors are there might be a weapon that will never break when used in this "machine"...

[note]BTW you need to be at > 75% to run it.[/note]
Ender eyes are available at HV in the chemical reactor. Combine some ender pearls with blaze powder.

NAND is used for data sticks, data orbs, and UV circuits. NOR chips are needed for data orbs and LuV circuits. You can get these by cutting NAND and NOR wafers. Just worry about the data sticks for now.

There are a number of chemical recipes that are unlocked with an §5HV Chemical Reactor§r; you will finally be able to make Ender Eyes, Ammonia, Gelled Toluene, and plenty of other useful components.

Alternatively, if you're up for it, you can head over to the Mass Processing tab and build yourself a §5Large Chemical Reactor§r (LCR) instead! In addition to having a more efficient overclock, the LCR can input multiple liquids so you won't need to mess with those pesky cells any longer.

[note]If you choose to go the LCR route, be sure to take a look at the PTFE questline just ahead of this one, starting with "Teflon Don".[/note]

You'll use this a lot to make high tier circuits, and for other recipes.

[note]You'll soon find that everything you use the PLE on needs a cleanroom, so make sure you start on that.[/note]
Some machines need emitters and sensors, like the analyzer or the circuit assembler.

Phobos has new ores, go get them!

[note]Honestly, aside from the dungeon, Phobos has all the ores you need.[/note]
An EV assembler is required to make lapotron crystals, coils, and other stuff.
This is the Nano Processor circuit line. Similar to the previous circuit line, these are four circuits. The §onano§r circuits go all the way to the LuV-tier Nano Mainframe.
Ammonium Chloride is used to precipitate the dissolved Platinum into a purer form, you'll need some of this.

What, recycling? That's lame. I just wanna void this stuff! - is what you might think right now, but be aware that Platinum Salt still holds a high concentration of Platinum. We can recycle it to get even more Platinum out of it!

You need to sift it to get Refined Platinum Salt. You only have a 95% chance to get a dust.

Your good old singleblock Molecular Transformer is gone. So how to obtain Sunnarium? Well there are a couple of ways, but at EV you'll need to radiate Glowstone and convert to Sunnarium.

The Glowstone rod will slowly charge up if you put it next to any fuel rod in your IC2 reactor. The exact charging speed is (number of rods adjacent) * (floor(hull temperature/3000) + 1) per second. A dual rod will count as 2 rods and quad fuel rod will count as 4 rods for this purpose. The Glowstone rod will neither emit heat, nor cause other component to emit more heat. You can treat this rod as a reactor plating in the IC2 Reactor Planner.

As you can see, the speed is directly proportional to hull temperature and rods adjacent. At the very extreme you can fully charge up a Glowstone rod in less than half a minute and output one Sunnarium every 10 second (on average, since cells aren't charged at same speed due to different position), but that require very considerable resource to set up. It also involves a vacuum freezer to cooldown the coolant cells and running the reactor at 66000+ hull temperature. Balance your need and your resource situation wisely. In any case, if you just wish to charge it up FAST then here is a possible string for the IC2 Reactor Planner:

erp=bpO50cpsdtJElVqn4hObFJc0tiOE0JpEHYpqTxJHvyezjSKRjUuwgouXzNOlU4a3hFBRygM=

Replace Containment Reactor Plating with actual Glowstone rods and you should be good to go. You can also use fewer reactor chambers by using less plating. Enjoy!
Place your solution in your Chemical Reactor to heat it while it spins. That way you get a dust out of your Solution, 100% lump free!

Monazite europium?

Unfortunately you won't be able to process it until you actually have some actual europium, so just hold onto this for now.
You have been using a very basic soldering alloy for a while now. Time for an upgrade! You will need it in your Assembly Line.

Here you have two options: Either you can mix some water with your nitrated mixture for relatively low yield, or you can mix it with the cerium-containing mixture you get from ores for double the amount.
You'll need to add even more nitric acid to be able to deal with the dust further.
Since you have concentrated your solution, it's now time to gather the solid Osmium from it. Go ahead and mix it with Hydrochloric Acid.
Hmm, the sifting did well, you lost a lot of impurities, but also, new ones from the sifting grates are there... Time to filter them out with water.

To make these chips you need Europium, as well as an HV Autoclave in the Asteroids dimension (or on your space station) to achieve "low gravity".

[note]Alternatively you can use an Industrial Autoclave.[/note]

If you want some of this highly mysterious material, you'll need to go to Enceladus and collect some of those crystals lying around everywhere and macerate them. Or grab some Ice with a shovel and get the Dust out of it. You can also turn the dust back into Unknown Crystal Shards with an Autoclave if you need those.

Now that you've completed the Netherite line and successfully set up your Mk1 Fusion Reactor, you finally have access to §5§lBoron Plasma§r, which is necessary for creating all of the different §4§lNetherite materials§r.

These will be important for the rest of the game and are used often in a variety of items and materials, so it's important to make upgrades to your line both in terms of power and recipe usage as you continue to progress through the tiers.

[note]UHV power and the QFT are the biggest milestones in terms of recipe upgrades for the line.[/note]

[warn]You must use a High-Current Industrial (Plasma) Arc Furnace, a regular one isn't powerful enough.[/warn]

§4Netherite Nanoparticles§r can be made from §5§lMacerating§r your newly attained Brittle Netherite Scraps.

[note]Almost seems too easy doesn't it?[/note]
Fluorine is one of the most important resources. You can recycle most fluorine from it.

You have two options to deal with it.

1) React with Sodium Dust in a chemical reactor.

2) Mix it with Indium Phosphate Dust in a mixer.

§nOne§r of the ways you will be using the Poor Nether Waste is to combine it with the Hellish Mud you obtained earlier in the §5§lMixer§r to create §4Rich Nether Waste§r.

We will be using the Rich Nether Waste for the next step, and it will continue to be valuable for a while, though it does eventually become unecessary to the line when you reach §4§lUHV.

IT COMES BACK AGAINNNNN? ! ! !

[note]You can find out more about the Neutron Activator and the Precise Assembler in the Multiblock tab.[/note]

If you want to color all those fancy cables and such, you'll want to use the humble Paintbrush. To uncolor them, use a bucket of water.

Alternatively, you can also use the IC2 Painter, GT Spray Can, or the AE2 Color Applicator.

[note]The buildcraft paintbrush and GT spray cans can now color many cables or pipes in a row with just a single click![/note]

Woah, a new metal?

By combining the forces of your newly created §2Enriched Naquqdah§r and §2Prismatic Acid§r in the §5§lAlloy Blast Smelter§r, you find that a new metal forms; §3Prismatic Naquadah§r.

[warn]This extremely strong, yet shockingly pliant material will be used in quite a few different structures and materials going forth.[/warn]

Just Make It ™.

[note]PS: You can make the ZPM CA now and start on the wetware line if you want, but the quests are in UV since you can't make the last circuit until then.[/note]

We can process Crystal Processing Units in a Laser Engraver to get the Crystal SoC. But as our demand for circuits gradually increases, this method of production is becoming untenable. We need a more advanced way to mass produce Crystal SoCs!

For that we use a new material, Cerium-doped Lutetium Aluminium Garnet (Ce:LuAG). LuAG has better performance when making Crystal SoCs - in fact it is a laser crystal material.

First, mix Lutetium, Aluminium, Oxygen, and a little Cerium to get the material blend. Then put the blend with Molten Lutetium into an Autoclave to get a regular LuAG gem. If you have already got Oganesson you can also directly use that. As the second step, use the LuAG gem and more of the blend to produce Exquisite LuAG Gems.

After you get the first gem, you can also use your Autoclave to grow crystals. Using the Vacuum Furnace might be a better choice to produce Exquisite LuAG Gems.

Lutetium, Lutetium, it needs a lot of Lutetium. How will you get a lot of Lutetium? Check out the Powerful Nuclear Physics quest tab!

You need Quantum Power ICs for UEV -> UIV and UIV -> UMV transformers, and UHV Hatches and Dynamos.

[note]You'll need a UHV Cutting Machine, or an OC'd multi to actually cut the wafers.[/note]

While one reactor is nice and all, why not make another? This time you can use the MK3 americium recipe to make it easier.

Costing just 1 ingot of Plutonium, and 2 cells of hydrogen, this recipe is much easier and faster than the MK2 version. By making americium plasma, then putting it through a turbine, you will get americium in the liquid state. You will need at least 132 plates for the controller and the fusion machine casings.

Plus, free power!

Now that you have Infinity, you can use the Avaritia Neutron Collector to get Piles of Neutrons. It does not use any power.

[note]Warning: This machine is extremely slow, so you might want to use something to help with that...[/note]

The next material is Infinity Catalyst. Infinity Catalyst needs to be compressed with insane amounts of force. The only machine capable of doing that is a Neutronium Compressor. Check out the Neutronium Compressor quest in Multiblock Goals.

To make the Catalyst, go to Vega B to get the dust. Then use the Neutronium Compressor to make Infinity Catalyst with 64 of the dust.

You can also do the insane Avaritia recipe to make the Infinity Catalyst, if you want a challenge :P

[note]The compressor can also be used to make singularities, culminating in the Eternal Singularity, used in the alternate recipe.[/note]

[note]If you want to automate singularity creation you can just click with the middle mouse button to modify the number of items in a pattern beyond the normal limits.[/note]

Welcome to Highly Ultimate Voltage... you're starting to get near the late game. In this tier you will use Cosmic Neutronium to create strong parts, and eventually start making your very own Infinity. You will gain access to some very powerful Quality-of-Life tools during this chapter.

[note]Oh, and... have fun with Xenoxene![/note]

Let's make a brand new plastic! The trees that you found on Barnada C have very interesting properties. Still, you can't just use the extracted radox substance raw. Some serious refining steps are in order!

§6You'll need it for a ton of stuff like UIV and UMV superconductors, UEV+ hatches, or parts of the Dyson Swarm.§r

Compared to the previous plastics, most of the steps required here are not chemical reactions. They are also very slow, so you might want to use a mega multiblock to help with that. This production line also underlines the necessity of large-scale plasma production. You will only need more going forward.

[note]Super Heavy Radox will be needed for Dimensionally Shifted Superfluid, so store some for later.[/note]

[note]The Heavy Radox you get as a byproduct can be used for MkV Naquadah Fuel.[/note]

This quest goes over all of the covers, just in case you forgot or they weren't mentioned. If they have their own quest, look at it for more info. You can put them on basically any GT block, pipe, machine, tank, etc. Though that doesn't consider whether it will work right or be useful. Shift-right-click will open a GUI for many of them. For covers that you can adjust the amount of, clicking the left side with a screwdriver goes down, and right goes up. All covers block rain on the side they're attached to. Generally you can adjust covers with a screwdriver, whether they have a GUI or not. You can remove them with a crowbar if you forgot.

§lFluid-Related Covers:§r
Pump: Transfers fluids at the speed specified on the tooltip.

Fluid Regulator: Regulates the rate that fluid can go through it, as well as pumping it. Can work x/s or x/t.

Steam Valve: Moves more, but is only for steam. Only goes to IV. Does not work on SH steam.

Overflow: Voids anything over the specified amount in the tank.

Fluid Detector: Will emit a redstone signal (RS) based on fluid fill rate of the tank it's on. By default, empty=15, full=0. Use inverted mode to swap. Useful on tanks and hatches.

Ender Fluid Link: Cover version of ender tanks to TP fluid around. Doesn't connect to ender tanks though.

Drain Module: Protects from rain on that side, and will fill the tank with water from rain if it can. Useless.

Fluid Filter: Filters fluid going through it. Click with a cell to set it.

Shutter: Blocks items/fluids when active. Use it with a machine controller and RS. Does not visually disconnect/block. You place it between the pipes, and put the MC on the same pipe.

§lItem-Related Covers:§r
Conveyor: Sends items. Use an item pipe in import mode or on a GT block/machine.

Robot Arm: Sends from a specific slot in the machine it's on (internal) to another specific slot in a block next to it (adjacent). Keep going through numbers until you get it right, it seems to change per machine. If the output slot is full, it won't output. There need to be a valid block with slots to send to or the settings won't save.

Item Detector: Will emit RS based on item fill rate of the block it's on. By default, empty=0, full=15. Detects by filled slots, so doesn't work right with the super/quantum chests. Probably better for buses.

Item Filter: This one is work-in-progress that is not yet finished. Feel free to contribute.

§lEU-Related Covers:§r
Energy Detector: Will emit RS based on EU fill rate of the batbuff/tank it's on. By default, empty=0, full=15. Make sure to set it to include batteries. Can also detect steam for some reason.

Power Pass Upgrade: Read the tooltip, then ask Bart how it works, he didn't bother telling anyone. He'll probably just tell you the code is the best documentation, just like the Tectech guys. May not actually be finished/work, either.

Tesla Coil/Tesla Coil Rich Edition: Use with the Tesla Tower to send EU wirelessly or something.

§lOther Covers:§r
Player Detector: Detects players. About 10 block range. [warn]Currently owner/other is inverted on non-pipes due to some bug or another.[/warn]

Activity Detector: Detects either recipe progress or idleness. 0%=0 RS, ~95%=14 by default. In idle mode, idleness is 15, and is 0 when running by default.

Machine Controller: Turns machine on/off based on RS. Disable machine will keep it off regardless of RS state. Safe Mode only works on MBs. If a MB runs out of power, it will normally turn off, but a MC will turn it back on, continuing to void stuff. This will set it to the disable machine state instead. You will also get a notification. [warn]Safe mode is currently broken?[/warn]

Needs Maintenance: Emits RS if it meets the condition specified. Rotor=turbine?

Redstone Transmitter/Receiver (In/Out): OK, these things are confusing, so listen closely. First, set a frequency, either by using the GUI, or using the original way: right-clicking it with an item. Empty hand = 0, other items = their item ID. Be careful not to reset it to 0 by accident. Honestly, just use the GUI. You can set it to private or not, it's the same kind of thing as EnderStorage, so you don't blare RS across the server. Channel state = RS level.

RT(O) sets the channel state based on the redstone level sent from outside to the specific side. You can TP an RS from this to the RR(O).
RT(I) sets the channel state based on redstone level that would be emitted from the machine itself. [warn]Always sends 15, so it's useless.[/warn]
RR(O) outputs redstone level based on channel state. You can TP RS from the RT(O) to here.
RR(I) controls whether a machine can work based on channel state, just like a machine controller cover does. If it has 0 RS, it stays off. At least 1, and it turns on. You send the signal from elsewhere with the RT(O).
RR(I) can also be used in conjunction with other covers whose states talk about conditional.

IC2 Heat vents: Speeds up machines a tiny amount? Also looks neat. TODO: someone please look at the code to see what it actually does.

Crafting Table: Adds a crafting table to the block you can use. Mostly cosmetic.

Planks/Plates/Foils: Blocks I/O on that side. Use a screwdriver to set allowed types of stuff through. Blocks everything by default. Set the output to another side first to avoid changing its settings too. You'll probably 'use' this by accident at least once.

Computer Monitor: Adds a visual look. Cosmetic-only cover.

Lens: Cosmetic-only cover.

You can also use AE2 facades as cosmetic covers.

These are the first parts you will need for your beloved nano armor.

[note]To actually complete the nano armor you will need EV circuits and nano crystals (see Storing and Transforming EU).[/note]

You can stop worrying about some mobs for now. This will help you survive a bit and will give you some of that SWAG. Your journey doesn't stop here traveller, as quantum armor awaits you!

[note]PS: The nano suit also acts as hazmat gear.[/note]
This special pair of goggles recharges itself while you wander in sunlight. How convenient.
The #1 product for spies and wanna-be-spies. Nano-Goggles. Don't you want one of these too? But they are sold out? Craft some!

The nano helmet is a bit special. It will provide you with night vision as long as you have energy!

[note]If you got these from a lootbag, hold these in your hand after completing the "Carbon Parts" quest to skip this quest.[/note]

Now that you are MV, you can make an Adventure Backpack. This very useful backpack can do many things. It can store 2 tools for quick access, has internal tanks for containing liquids - use the Hose to access them, a built in crafting table, and storage.

When placed on your back, it can be accessed using a quick-access key, default is B. Check your controls for details.

When placed on the ground, it can be accessed with a right-click. While on the ground, the bedroll can be deployed and used.

They can also be upgraded to add special abilities. For a full list, visit [url]https://wiki.gtnewhorizons.com/wiki/Adventure_Backpacks[/url].

[note]It uses up the same slot as the Coal Jetpack and the Copterpack, so you'll either need to choose one, or swap them around.[/note]
You probably have a ton of items floating around in your base. So why not use them to kill your enemies?

Very similar to the LCE in most aspects, the Extreme Combustion Engine was created to close the gap between the diesels and more advanced forms of powergen, mainly nuclear fusion. However, the main thing they differ on is the fuel that they can run, and in this case you're limited to the powerful fuel from the last tier, High Octane Gasoline.

That's right, only this fuel will work, and in exchange the ECE will output more than 10k EU/t. If you add Liquid Oxygen, which you'll need 4 Vacuum Freezers for (3 at HV and 1 at EV), you can keep it boosted permanently, which will send it up to 4 IV amps of power and 150% fuel efficiency. It's in your best interest to keep this thing going if you want to keep using Oil fuels for power, and the only thing you can rely on is your automated HOG setup, which you might have to, and should improve with the new IV multis as soon as you can, unless you have other sources of power working simultaneously.

As a last note, there is also an IV equivalent of the Vacuum Freezer, if you want to tackle the Liquid Oxygen needs that way. It's more power efficient, look it up on the GT++ tab.

Another HV multi, and another Stainless Steel sink. Not only that, but it needs 16 coils to work! There is a quest about this multi in the Multiblock Goals tab, as per usual.

Despite being expensive, this is an important multiblock to build. It does the cracking recipes like the Chemical Reactor does, and you might even be tempted to make an LCR for this purpose, since it's cheap and can do many recipes at once, but the Oil Cracker is much more efficient and gives several bonuses when used (check the tooltip for more information). As it stands, it's a higher initial investment that will pay off in the long run, many times over, and in this pack everything takes a long time, so get on it!

At this point, you have almost all the additives needed to transform basic gasoline into the powerful HOG. What you'll need to do next is to create the gasoline itself, and this begins with Raw Gasoline. It has four different inputs, and each of them is sourced in a different way, but none of them are new: Naphtha and Refinery gas are well-known to you, Methanol was created in the previous quest and Acetone was explained in the "Acetone and Ethenone" quest in HV.

An important detail here is that Raw Gasoline is 80% Naphtha. It can be obtained in large quantities from raw oil. If you are not pumping raw oil, you will most likely find the amount you get to be short. But this problem has already been solved! Your source of Octane, if processed in a DT as explained, will also crack the Light Fuel into a lot of Naphtha that you can use here to compensate for the low amount you get from Oil. If you get low on Light Fuel, you can also crack Heavy Fuel to get some.
